annotations.label: Ordinary differential equation

anchor.snippet: an ordinary differential equation ( ODE ) is a differential equation (DE) dependent on only a single independent variable

annotations.note: Mathlib lacks an ODE type per se; the closest formalization is `IsIntegralCurve γ v` expressing that γ solves the first-order autonomous-looking ODE γ' t = v t (γ t).

annotations.label: Linear differential equation

anchor.snippet: A linear differential equation is a differential equation that is defined by a linear polynomial in the unknown function and its derivatives

annotations.note: No definition of linear differential equation as a predicate or structure is present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Newton's second law as ODE

anchor.snippet: A simple example is Newton's second law of motion

annotations.note: Newton's second law is not formalized as a worked ODE example in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Explicit ODE of order n

anchor.snippet: is called an explicit ordinary differential equation of order

annotations.note: Only the first-order case is formalized via `IsIntegralCurve`; there is no general explicit order-n ODE definition.

annotations.label: Implicit ODE of order n

anchor.snippet: an implicit ordinary differential equation of order

annotations.note: Mathlib has no definition of an implicit ODE of order n.

annotations.label: Autonomous differential equation

anchor.snippet: A differential equation is autonomous if it does not depend on the variable x .

annotations.note: No predicate isolating the autonomous case (vector field independent of t) exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Linear differential equation (classification)

anchor.snippet: A differential equation is linear if

annotations.note: Mathlib does not formalize the classification of an ODE as linear.

annotations.label: Homogeneous linear differential equation

anchor.snippet: A linear differential equation is homogeneous if

annotations.note: No definition for a homogeneous linear ODE is present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Nonhomogeneous linear differential equation

anchor.snippet: A linear differential equation is nonhomogeneous if

annotations.note: No definition for a nonhomogeneous linear ODE is present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Nonlinear differential equation

anchor.snippet: A differential equation that is not linear.

annotations.note: No definition for nonlinear ODEs is present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Explicit system of ODEs

anchor.snippet: is an explicit system of ordinary differential equations of order

annotations.note: An integral curve in a normed vector space E covers first-order systems of ODEs, but order-n systems are not formalized.

annotations.label: Differential algebraic equations

anchor.snippet: implicit ODE systems with a singular Jacobian are termed differential algebraic equations

annotations.note: Differential algebraic equations are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Solution / integral curve

anchor.snippet: is called a solution or integral curve

annotations.note: `IsIntegralCurveOn γ v s` directly defines an integral curve of the time-dependent vector field v on s.

annotations.label: Extension of a solution

anchor.snippet: is called an extension of

annotations.note: There is no predicate for extending an integral curve to a larger interval in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Maximal solution

anchor.snippet: A solution that has no extension is called a maximal solution

annotations.note: Maximal solutions of ODEs are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Global solution

anchor.snippet: A solution defined on all of

annotations.note: `IsIntegralCurve γ v` is precisely an integral curve defined on all of ℝ, matching the notion of a global solution.

annotations.label: General solution

anchor.snippet: A general solution of an

annotations.note: The notion of a general solution parameterised by n arbitrary constants is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Particular solution

anchor.snippet: A particular solution is derived from the general solution by setting the constants to particular values

annotations.note: No formal definition of particular solution exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Singular solution

anchor.snippet: A singular solution is a solution that cannot be obtained by assigning definite values to the arbitrary constants

annotations.note: Singular solutions are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Solutions of finite duration

anchor.snippet: it is possible under some conditions to develop solutions of finite duration

annotations.note: Finite-duration solutions are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Finite duration solution example

anchor.snippet: Admits the finite duration solution

annotations.note: This explicit finite-duration example is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Sturm–Liouville theory

anchor.snippet: Sturm–Liouville theory is a theory of a special type of second-order linear ordinary differential equation

annotations.note: There is no Sturm–Liouville theory file or definition in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Peano existence theorem

anchor.snippet: Peano existence theorem

annotations.note: Mathlib formalizes Picard–Lindelöf existence under a Lipschitz hypothesis but not Peano's continuity-only existence theorem.

annotations.label: Picard–Lindelöf theorem

anchor.snippet: Picard–Lindelöf theorem

annotations.note: The Picard–Lindelöf (Cauchy–Lipschitz) theorem is proved in `IsPicardLindelof.exists_eq_forall_mem_Icc_hasDerivWithinAt`.

annotations.label: Local existence and uniqueness theorem

anchor.section: Local existence and uniqueness theorem simplified
anchor.snippet: are continuous in a closed rectangle

annotations.note: Local existence is given by `IsPicardLindelof.exists_eq_forall_mem_Icc_hasDerivWithinAt` and uniqueness by `ODE_solution_unique` in `Mathlib.Analysis.ODE.Gronwall`.

anchors.section: Local existence and uniqueness theorem simplified
anchors.snippet: are continuous in a closed rectangle

annotations.label: Global uniqueness and maximum domain

anchor.section: Global uniqueness and maximum domain of solution
anchor.snippet: there exists a unique maximum (possibly infinite) open interval

annotations.note: Global uniqueness for Lipschitz vector fields is given by `ODE_solution_unique_univ`, but the existence of a maximal interval of definition is not formalized.

annotations.label: Reduction to a first-order system

anchor.snippet: can be written as a system of

annotations.note: Mathlib does not formalize the order-reduction transformation from an order-n ODE to a first-order system.

annotations.label: Guessing solutions method

anchor.snippet: it is sometimes possible to solve a DE simply by guessing the solution and validating it is correct

annotations.note: The informal 'guess and verify' technique is not formalized as a theorem in Mathlib.
